text.title: HTTP Error Mapping and Panic Recovery
text.summary: Centralised handler error mapper, panic-recovery middleware, and validation aggregation.

rule.statement: Log at exactly one place per error — the outermost handler. Intermediate layers wrap and return; never log-then-return (produces duplicate log lines per request).
rule.rationale: If every layer logs on error, a single failed DB query produces 3-4 identical log lines per request. The handler is the only layer with enough context (request path, user, correlation ID) to log meaningfully.

rule.statement: Panic recovery middleware must capture runtime/debug.Stack() before returning 500. Stripping the stack trace to "clean up logs" hides the root cause of production panics.
rule.rationale: Panic recovery without a stack trace produces a log line with no actionable information. The stack trace is the primary diagnostic for production panics.

section.title: Validation Aggregation
section.p: Use errors.Join (Go 1.20+) or a MultiError for collecting all validation failures before returning. Returning on the first error forces clients to fix one field at a time.
